page: Microsoft Azure D8s_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv6 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor includes a large 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions.

This server type runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and does not include local storage or onboard GPUs. However, it is designated as Premium Storage capable, allowing users to attach high-performance external block storage. The lack of local storage and GPU hardware makes this instance highly specialized for workloads that rely on network-attached storage and CPU-bound processing rather than local disk I/O or hardware-accelerated graphics.

In benchmark testing, the D8s_v6 demonstrates strong single-threaded performance, particularly in gzip compression and Geekbench text processing, alongside a strong Geekbench single-core score of 2162.0. Multi-core performance is average, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 7990.85 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 8304.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 621.59 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 67.85 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. The instance shows limitations in complex AI workloads, registering a weak performance of 7.94 tokens/sec for text generation on a medium 7B LLM.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D8s_v6 offers a balanced resource density suitable for standard enterprise applications without the premium cost associated with dedicated local NVMe drives or GPU accelerators. It is best suited for general-purpose workloads such as web serving, application servers, database hosting using remote storage, text processing, and lightweight software compilation.
description: The Microsoft Azure D8s_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs but is Premium Storage capable for external block storage. Benchmark results show strong single-core performance, particularly in text processing and single-threaded gzip compression, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier. It exhibits weak performance for medium-sized LLM text generation. Qualitatively, the D8s_v6 provides a cost-effective, balanced hardware profile for workloads such as web serving, application hosting, and text processing that do not require local NVMe disks or GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8s_v6 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and Premium Storage capability, offering strong single-core performance for standard workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8s_v6 is an x86_64 general-purpose VM with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and Premium Storage support for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, strong single-core performance, and external premium storage compatibility.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM and a 260 MB L3 cache
Premium Storage capable with no local storage or onboard GPUs
Strong single-core performance in text processing and gzip compression
Best suited for web serving, application hosting, and text processing
